1540	
1541	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1542	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1543	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1544	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1545	
1546	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1547	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1548	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1549	`,
1550			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
1551	  ddx agent execute-loop
1552	
1553	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
1554	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once
1555	
1556	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
1557	  ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval 30s
1558	
1559	  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
1560	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness codex
1561	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7`,
1562			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
1563			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteLoop,
1564		}
1565		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
1566		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
1567		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
1568		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
1569		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
1570		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
1571		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
1572		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
1573		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
1574		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
1575		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
1576		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; execute-loop always runs inline (ADR-022)")
1577		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "execute-loop always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
1578		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (e.g. for doc-only beads or tight iteration loops)")
1579		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness to use for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
1580		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
1581		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop the loop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited; subscription and local providers do not count")
1582		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout (e.g. 60m, 2h); overrides project config and model-class defaults. Use when a thinking model needs more than the default 15 min.")
1583		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
1584		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
1585		return cmd
1586	}
1587	
1588	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoop(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
1589		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, false)
1590	}
1591	
1592	// hostnameOrEmpty returns os.Hostname() or "" on error. Used to populate
1593	// the workerprobe identity envelope without short-circuiting probe startup
1594	// on a transient hostname-lookup failure.
1595	func hostnameOrEmpty() string {
1596		h, err := os.Hostname()
1597		if err != nil {
1598			return ""
1599		}
1600		return h
1601	}
1602	
1603	// runAgentExecuteLoopImpl is the shared implementation for runAgentExecuteLoop
1604	// and runWork. When treatPassthroughAsOpaque is true (ddx work path),
1605	// harness/provider/model are passed to Execute unchanged and
1606	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
1607	// branch on their values. When false (ddx agent execute-loop path), the
1608	// pre-flight validation gate runs first.
1609	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd *cobra.Command, treatPassthroughAsOpaque bool) error {
1610		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
1611		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
1612		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
1613		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
1614		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
1615		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
1616		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
1617		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
1618		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
1619		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
1620		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
1621		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
1622		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
1623		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1624		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1625		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1626		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
1627		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
1628		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
1629	
1630		// Zero-config auto-route: when the operator passes no routing flags and
1631		// the project has no .ddx/config.yaml routing block, dispatch flows
1632		// through fizeau's provider resolution. Provider configuration and any
1633		// "no providers configured" error reporting is fizeau's concern; ddx
1634		// does not read fizeau's config files.
1635		//
1636		// ddx-b2c9a245: tier is inferred per bead via escalation.InferTier in
1637		// the executor closure below when no routing flags are present.
1638		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
1639			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
1640		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
1641	
1642		// ADR-022: execute-loop always runs inline. The legacy --local flag is
1643		// a deprecated no-op and the server-submit path is removed from this CLI
1644		// surface — server-spawned workers exec `ddx work` directly.
1645	
1646		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1647		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1648		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1649		// any bead status changes hands.
1650		// Skipped for ddx work (treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true): harness/provider/model
1651		// are opaque passthrough constraints there; DDx does not validate them.
1652		if !treatPassthroughAsOpaque {
1653			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
1654				return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1655			}
1656		}
1657	
1658		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1659	
1660		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
1661		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
1662		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
1663		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
1664		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
1665		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
1666		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
1667		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
1668		var loopSink io.Writer
1669		if f, err := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); err == nil {
1670			loopSink = f
1671			defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
1672		}
1673	
1674		// ADR-022 step 2: spin up the worker server-probe + best-effort event
1675		// mirror. The probe is autonomous-friendly: if no ddx-server is
1676		// reachable, it buffers events into a 200-cap ring; on
1677		// NotConnected→Connected transitions it registers and flushes the
1678		// backfill. Per ADR-022 the worker keeps working regardless of probe
1679		// state, so this code never blocks the loop on network I/O.
1680		probeIdent := workerprobe.Identity{
1681			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
1682			Harness:      harness,
1683			Model:        model,
1684			ExecutorPID:  os.Getpid(),
1685			ExecutorHost: hostnameOrEmpty(),
1686			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
1687		}
1688		probe := workerprobe.New(probeIdent, workerprobe.Config{
1689			AddrFunc: serverpkg.ReadServerAddr,
1690		})
1691		probeCtx, probeCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1692		probe.Start(probeCtx)
1693		defer func() {
1694			probeCancel()
1695			probe.Stop()
1696		}()
1697		if loopSink != nil {
1698			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(loopSink, probe)
1699		} else {
1700			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(io.Discard, probe)
1701		}
1702	
1703		// Start session log tailer so the user sees progress during agent execution
1704		tailCtx, tailCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1705		go agent.TailSessionLogs(tailCtx, projectRoot, cmd.OutOrStdout())
1706	
1707		// Instantiate a process-local LandCoordinator for the inline land path.
1708		// Stopped on function exit.
1709		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
1710		defer localCoord.Stop()
1711	
1712		// Build post-merge reviewer unless --no-review is set.
1713		// Reviewer is on-by-default: runs at smart tier after every successful merge.
1714		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
1715		if !noReview {
1716			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
1717				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1718				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1719				BeadEvents:  bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1720				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1721				Model:       reviewModel,
1722			}
1723		}
1724	
1725		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
1726	
1727		// Cost-cap state shared across attempts for this loop run.
1728		// paths. Accumulated billed spend (excluding local and subscription
1729		// providers) above --max-cost trips the cap and halts further bead
1730		// claiming. See escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD / CountsTowardCostCap.
1731		costCap := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(maxCostUSD, func(harnessName string) bool {
1732			// Resolve the harness's billing class via the service. Treat any
1733			// resolution error as "count by default" (safe — we'd rather cap
1734			// early than silently overshoot).
1735			svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
1736			if svcErr != nil {
1737				return true
1738			}
1739			infos, err := svc.ListHarnesses(context.Background())
1740			if err != nil {
1741				return true
1742			}
1743			for _, h := range infos {
1744				if h.Name == harnessName {
1745					return escalation.CountsTowardCostCap(h.IsLocal, h.IsSubscription, h.CostClass)
1746				}
1747			}
1748			return true
1749		})
1750		accumulateBilledCost := func(report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
1751			costCap.Add(report.Harness, report.CostUSD)
1752		}
1753		costCapTripped := func() (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, bool) {
1754			detail, tripped := costCap.Tripped()
1755			if !tripped {
1756				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, false
1757			}
1758			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1759				Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
1760				Detail: detail,
1761			}, true
1762		}
1763	
1764		// singleTierAttempt runs one execution attempt with an explicit harness
1765		// and model. It is called both by the non-escalating path and by each
1766		// iteration of the tier escalation loop.
1767		singleTierAttempt := func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, tier escalation.ModelTier, resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1768			gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
1769			attemptProvider := provider
1770			if resolvedProvider != "" {
1771				attemptProvider = resolvedProvider
1772			}
1773	
1774			// ddx-b2c9a245: when zero-config auto-route is active, derive the
1775			// per-attempt profile from the inferred tier rather than the static
1776			// "always cheap" fallback the previous hot-fix used.
1777			attemptProfile := profile
1778			if autoInferTier && tier != "" {
1779				attemptProfile = escalation.TierToProfile(tier)
1780			}
1781	
1782			loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
1783				Harness:           resolvedHarness,
1784				Model:             resolvedModel,
1785				Provider:          attemptProvider,
1786				ModelRef:          modelRef,
1787				Profile:           attemptProfile,
1788				Effort:            effort,
1789				MinPower:          minPower,
1790				MaxPower:          maxPower,
1791				OpaquePassthrough: treatPassthroughAsOpaque,
1792			}
1793			if requestTimeout > 0 {
1794				loopOverrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
1795			}
1796			attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, loopOverrides)
1797			res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
1798				FromRev:    fromRev,
1799				BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1800			}, gitOps)
1801			if execErr != nil && res == nil {
1802				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
1803			}
1804			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
1805				landReq := agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res)
1806				landRes, landErr := localCoord.Submit(landReq)
1807				if landErr == nil {
1808					agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)
1809				} else if execErr == nil {
1810					execErr = landErr
1811				}
1812			} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
1813				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
1814				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
1815			} else if res != nil && res.ExitCode != 0 {
1816				res.Outcome = "preserved"
1817				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
1818			}
1819			if execErr != nil {
1820				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
1821			}
1822			tierStr := ""
1823			if tier != "" {
1824				tierStr = string(tier)
1825			}
1826			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1827				BeadID:             res.BeadID,
1828				AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
1829				WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
1830				Harness:            res.Harness,
1831				Provider:           res.Provider,
1832				Model:              res.Model,
1833				ActualPower:        res.ActualPower,
1834				Tier:               tierStr,
1835				Status:             res.Status,
1836				Detail:             res.Detail,
1837				SessionID:          res.SessionID,
1838				BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
1839				ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
1840				PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
1841				NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
1842				CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
1843				DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
1844			}, nil
1845		}
1846	
1847		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
1848			Store:    store,
1849			Reviewer: reviewer,
1850			Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1851				// Stop AT THE START of each new bead claim if the cost cap has
1852				// already tripped — otherwise we'd burn one extra attempt before
1853				// halting the queue.
1854				if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1855					cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1856					return cappedReport, nil
1857				}
1858				// Pass operator intent (harness/provider/model) directly to
1859				// Execute via singleTierAttempt; do not pre-resolve the route
1860				// (CONTRACT-003 / ddx-da19756a).
1861				//
1862				// ddx-b2c9a245: under zero-config auto-route, infer the tier
1863				// from bead metadata (labels, kind, scope) and pass it through
1864				// so singleTierAttempt selects the matching profile.
1865				var inferredTier escalation.ModelTier
1866				if autoInferTier {
1867					if b, getErr := store.Get(beadID); getErr == nil {
1868						inferredTier = escalation.InferTier(b)
1869					} else {
1870						inferredTier = escalation.TierCheap
1871					}
1872				}
1873				report, err := singleTierAttempt(ctx, beadID, inferredTier, harness, provider, model)
1874				if err == nil {
1875					accumulateBilledCost(report)
1876					if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1877						cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1878						return cappedReport, nil
1879					}
1880				}
1881				return report, err
1882			}),
1883		}
1884	
1885		// SD-024 Stage 1: dispatch flows through LoadAndResolve + RunWithConfig
1886		// so .ddx/config.yaml's durable knobs (review_max_retries,
1887		// no_progress_cooldown, max_no_changes_before_close, heartbeat_interval,
1888		// agent.harness/model/permissions, etc.) reach the running loop without
1889		// per-knob CLI plumbing. CLI flag values feed CLIOverrides which win
1890		// over the on-disk config when set.
1891		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
1892			Assignee: resolveClaimAssignee(),
1893			Harness:  harness,
1894			Model:    model,
1895			Provider: provider,
1896			ModelRef: modelRef,
1897			Profile:  profile,
1898			Effort:   effort,
1899		}
1900		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
1901		if err != nil {
1902			// Surface the LoadAndResolve error rather than letting a
1903			// zero-value ResolvedConfig flow into RunWithConfig — the
1904			// SD-024 sealed-construction sentinel would otherwise panic
1905			// at first accessor read instead of producing a clean CLI error.
1906			tailCancel()
1907			return fmt.Errorf("load resolved config: %w", err)
1908		}
1909	
1910		cliLandingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
1911		result, err := worker.Run(cmd.Context(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
1912			Once:         once,
1913			PollInterval: pollInterval,
1914			Log:          cmd.OutOrStdout(),
1915			EventSink:    loopSink,
1916			WorkerID:     resolveClaimAssignee(),
1917			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
1918			SessionID:    loopSessionID,
1919			PreClaimHook: buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps),
1920			NoReview:     noReview,
1921		})
1922		tailCancel() // stop session log tailer
1923		if err != nil {
1924			return err
1925		}
1926	
1927		if asJSON {
1928			payload := struct {
1929				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
1930				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
1931			}{
1932				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
1933				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
1934			}
1935			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
1936			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
1937			return enc.Encode(payload)
1938		}
1939	
1940		if result.NoReadyWork {
1941			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "project: %s\n", projectRoot)
1942			fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "No execution-ready beads.")
1943			d := result.NoReadyWorkDetail
1944			if len(d.SkippedEpics) > 0 {
1945				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  skipped %d ready epic(s) (epics are structural containers; decompose into tasks): %s\n",
1946					len(d.SkippedEpics), strings.Join(d.SkippedEpics, ", "))
1947			}
1948			if len(d.SkippedOnCooldown) > 0 {
1949				retryHint := ""
1950				if d.NextRetryAfter != "" {
1951					retryHint = " (next retry-after: " + d.NextRetryAfter + ")"
1952				}
1953				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  skipped %d bead(s) on retry cooldown%s: %s\n",
1954					len(d.SkippedOnCooldown), retryHint, strings.Join(d.SkippedOnCooldown, ", "))
1955			}
1956			if len(d.SkippedNotEligible) > 0 {
1957				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  skipped %d bead(s) with execution-eligible=false: %s\n",
1958					len(d.SkippedNotEligible), strings.Join(d.SkippedNotEligible, ", "))
1959			}
1960			if len(d.SkippedSuperseded) > 0 {
1961				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  skipped %d superseded bead(s): %s\n",
1962					len(d.SkippedSuperseded), strings.Join(d.SkippedSuperseded, ", "))
1963			}
1964			return nil
1965		}
1966	
1967		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
1968		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "completed: %d  |  successes: %d  |  failures: %d\n", result.Attempts, result.Successes, result.Failures)
1969		if result.Failures > 0 {
1970			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "\nfailed:\n")
1971			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
1972				if attempt.Status != "success" {
1973					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
1974					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
1975						fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), " (preserved)")
1976					}
1977					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout())
1978				}
1979			}
1980		}
1981		return nil
1982	}

1984	// executeLoopWithServer submits an execute-loop job to the running ddx server.
1985	// The server starts a background worker and returns its ID.
1986	func (f *CommandFactory) executeLoopWithServer(cmd *cobra.Command, projectRoot, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort string, once bool, pollInterval time.Duration, asJSON bool, noReview bool, reviewHarness, reviewModel string, opaquePassthrough bool) error {
1987		serverBase := resolveServerURL(projectRoot)
1988	
1989		workerSpec := map[string]any{

reason: Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs.

## Why this attempt declined to commit

The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.

### Actual surface (vs stated estimate)

cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:

- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction
- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization
- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer`
- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation)
- in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService)
- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net
- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress`
- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS
- terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result
- BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes

Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:

- /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress
- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial)
- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging
- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent

### Test surface

cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:

- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or
- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).

This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates."

### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)

"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit"

Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper.

### Size-check verdict

- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.
- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).
- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.

The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.

## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)

Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):

### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing
Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)
Scope:
- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).
- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.
- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.
- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.
- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.
- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.
Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.
Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.

### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion
Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec
Scope:
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.
- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.
- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.
Estimated: ~300–500 LOC.

### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)
Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit
Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:
- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.
- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.
- POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once.
- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).
- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.
Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.
Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.

### Dependencies
- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)
- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)

## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit

The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.

## Bookkeeping

A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.",

data.text: This bead has a critical architectural conflict that requires human triage before proceeding. I'll write a needs_investigation rationale rather than committing in either direction.

25	type ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct {
26		// ProjectRoot overrides the manager's default project root for this worker.
27		// When set, the worker scans and executes beads from this project instead of
28		// the server's primary working directory. Must be an absolute path to a
29		// directory containing a .ddx/ folder. Validated by the server before the
30		// worker starts.
31		ProjectRoot  string        `json:"project_root,omitempty"`

data.text: I'm going to decline this attempt with a `needs_investigation` rationale. Two reasons make me pause: (1) architectural conflict with ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb), and (2) the bead exceeds the size-check thresholds.

input.content:
status: needs_investigation
reason: Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.
- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above.
- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

data.text:
Wrote `needs_investigation` rationale at `.ddx/executions/20260503T110552-7129c1c3/no_changes_rationale.txt`.

Two reasons for declining without committing:

1. **Architectural conflict** with open bead **ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7)**, whose acceptance is "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." This bead wants to UNIFY that struct; step 7 wants to DELETE it. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section is silent on step 7 — that's the gap. Also note: ADR-022 step 4 already neutered the `--local` flag, so the bead's stated user-observed symptom can't be reproduced via the CLI today (the cobra→server-submit branch is dead code; only the REST/GraphQL server-spawn paths remain in scope).

2. **Size-check fails on its own**: 17 ACs across cobra/HTTP/GraphQL/workers/tests/backward-compat/forward-compat/custom-Duration; touches >5 files comfortably >500 LOC including a literal sweep across 8+ workers_*_test.go files.

I deliberately did not decompose, because filing child beads would commit the project to the unification path before the conflict with step 7 is resolved by a human. The rationale lays out three options (drop in favor of step 7 / keep and supersede step 7 / sequence narrow-fix-then-step-7-then-close) for the orchestrator to pick from.
